Problem Statement: Solve Application with Ellipses
Question: A planet moves in an elliptical orbit around its sun. The closest the planet gets to the sun is approximately 10 AU and the furthest is approximately 30 AU. The sun is one of the foci of the elliptical orbit. Letting the ellipse center at the origin and labeling the axes in AU, the orbit will look like the figure below. Use the graph to write an equation for the elliptical orbit of the planet.

In your own words, define an ellipse and write the equation of an ellipse centered at the origin in standard form. Draw a sketch of the ellipse labeling the center, vertices and major and minor axes.
Explain in your own words how to get the axes from the equation in standard form.
Compare and contrast the graphs of the equations $$\frac{x^{2}}{4} + \frac{y^{2}}{9} = 1$$ and $$\frac{x^{2}}{9} + \frac{y^{2}}{4} = 1 .$$
Explain in your own words, the difference between a vertex and a focus of the ellipse.
